data.title: CometChat New Features: Build Real-Time Chat Into Any App
data.excerpt: CometChat's latest features make adding real-time chat, video calls, and AI moderation to your app easier than ever. See what's new and start building free.

$media.alt: CometChat New Features: Build Real-Time Chat Into Any App

children.text: *Disclosure: This post contains affiliate links. If you sign up through them, I may earn a commission at no extra cost to you.*

I have built enough side projects and client apps to know the pain: you want real-time chat inside your product, but the moment you open the "build it yourself" rabbit hole, you lose three weeks to WebSocket debugging, push notification quirks, and video call latency that makes users rage-quit. That is exactly the problem CometChat exists to solve — and they have been shipping features at a pace that deserves a proper walkthrough.
If you have not heard of
CometChat

before, think of it as a developer-first SDK that lets you bolt full-featured messaging, voice, and video onto any web or mobile app. The promise sounds simple: real-time communication in hours, not months. After spending time with their latest releases, I can tell you the promise holds up. Let me walk you through what is new, why it matters, and whether it is the right fit for your next build.
Check out CometChat's free developer plan here
Why Developers Keep Choosing CometChat
There are plenty of chat APIs on the market. What makes CometChat stick is a combination of depth and simplicity that is surprisingly rare.

First, the developer experience. Their SDKs cover React, React Native, Flutter, iOS (Swift), Android (Kotlin), and vanilla JavaScript. Installation takes minutes. The documentation is clean, with copy-paste snippets that actually work on the first try — I tested this with a React project last week and had a functioning one-on-one chat running in under 20 minutes. No exaggeration.

Second, the feature set goes far beyond basic text messaging. You get read receipts, typing indicators, threaded conversations, reactions, message editing and deletion, file and media sharing, group chats, and mentions. On top of that, voice and video calling come built in — no need to integrate a separate WebRTC provider. CometChat handles the media routing, call quality optimization, and even supports screen sharing and call recording.

Third, the infrastructure is genuinely global. They run on an edge network designed for low-latency delivery regardless of where your users are located. For any app with a geographically distributed audience, this is not a nice-to-have — it is table stakes.
If you want a deeper dive into the reasons to pick this platform, we covered it in detail in our
why choose CometChat
guide.
New Features Worth Getting Excited About
CometChat has not been sitting still. Several recent additions significantly raise the bar:
AI Agent Platform

— This is the headline feature. You can now bring your own AI agent directly into conversations. Imagine a customer support chat where an AI agent handles the first touch, answers common questions, and seamlessly hands off to a human when the issue gets complex. CometChat does not lock you into a specific AI model either — the "Bring Your Own Agent" approach means you plug in the LLM or bot framework you already trust. For SaaS products, this is a game-changer for support costs and response times.
AI-Powered Moderation

— Moderation is one of those problems every developer underestimates. Toxic messages, spam, harassment — they show up faster than you think. CometChat's AI moderation layer catches problematic content in real time, flagging or blocking it before other users see it. It goes beyond simple keyword filtering: the system understands context, which means fewer false positives and a healthier community without a full-time moderation team.
Advanced Notifications Suite

— Push notifications used to be a separate headache. CometChat now offers a multi-channel notification system that covers in-app alerts, push notifications (iOS and Android), and even email fallback. You can configure notification rules per user preference, per channel, and per message type. The granularity is impressive and means your users get alerted without getting spammed.
Insights and Analytics Dashboard

— You cannot improve what you do not measure. The new analytics dashboard gives you visibility into message volume, active users, engagement trends, and moderation actions. For product teams making data-driven decisions, this is the kind of built-in intelligence that eliminates the need for a separate analytics integration.
Enhanced Compliance and Security

— For apps in healthcare, fintech, or any regulated industry, CometChat has added deeper compliance tooling. End-to-end encryption, data residency controls, GDPR and HIPAA-aligned configurations — these were previously gaps that forced teams to look elsewhere. Not anymore.
Richer Search and Message Controls

— Full-text search across conversation history, advanced message pinning, scheduled messages, and improved admin controls give power users and community managers the tools they need without workarounds.

Each of these features ships as part of the existing SDK. No separate plugins, no extra vendor contracts. That unified approach is a big reason CometChat feels polished compared to stitching together three or four different services.
For a hands-on guide to getting the most out of these capabilities, see our
CometChat tips and best practices
Pricing That Won't Give You a Headache
One thing I genuinely appreciate about CometChat's approach: transparent pricing. No guessing games, no "contact sales for a quote" walls that waste your time.
They offer four clear tiers:
Build (Free)

— Full access to every feature for development and testing. Up to 100 users. No credit card required. This is not a crippled trial; it is a real sandbox where you can build and validate your integration before spending a dollar.
Basic ($239/month billed annually)
— Covers rich in-app chat, push notifications, basic moderation, and access to the global edge network. Sufficient for many early-stage apps and MVPs.
Advanced ($359/month billed annually)
— The most popular tier. Adds multi-channel notifications, AI moderation, analytics, and deeper controls. If you are building something meant for production scale, this is likely where you land.
Enterprise
— Custom pricing for apps needing dedicated infrastructure, advanced compliance, and priority support.

What stands out here is the predictability. You pick a plan based on monthly active users, and you know exactly what you pay. No surprise invoices from usage spikes. You can also toggle between monthly and annual billing, with a 20% savings on annual plans. Compare this to providers who meter per message or per API call — the bill shock with those models is real, and CometChat sidesteps it entirely.
Explore CometChat's pricing and start your free build plan
Who Is CometChat Built For?
CometChat is not just for one type of team. Here is where I see it delivering the most value:
Startup founders and indie developers

— If you are building a marketplace, social app, or community platform and chat is a core feature (not a nice-to-have sidebar), CometChat saves you months of engineering time. The free Build plan means you can prototype and pitch without upfront cost.
Product teams at mid-stage companies

— You already have an app. Your users are asking for messaging, or you want to add real-time support. The Advanced plan gives you production-ready infrastructure without hiring a dedicated real-time communications engineer.
EdTech platforms

— Virtual classrooms, tutoring marketplaces, cohort-based courses — they all need reliable video calling and chat. CometChat handles both in a single SDK, and the AI moderation helps keep conversations safe for younger audiences.
Healthcare and fintech apps

— With the enhanced compliance features, regulated industries can now adopt CometChat with confidence. Encrypted messaging, audit trails, and data residency options check the boxes that legal and security teams care about.
Marketplace and on-demand service apps

— Think ride-sharing, freelance platforms, or food delivery. The buyer-seller communication layer is critical, and CometChat's real-time messaging with notifications ensures conversations happen fast and do not get lost.
For a broader look at how different industries leverage this platform, our
CometChat use cases
breakdown covers it all.
How CometChat Compares to Building It Yourself
I want to be direct: building real-time chat from scratch is almost never the right call unless communication is literally your entire product (and even then, you should evaluate before committing).
Here is what DIY chat actually requires:
WebSocket or Socket.io server infrastructure
Message persistence and database design
Push notification services for iOS and Android
WebRTC setup for voice and video, including TURN/STUN servers
Moderation tooling
Scaling logic for concurrent connections
Compliance and encryption implementation
Ongoing maintenance as platforms and SDKs update

That is easily a three-to-six-month project for a competent team, and you have not even started on the UI. CometChat compresses all of this into an afternoon of integration work. The SDK handles the infrastructure, the protocols, the scaling, and the edge cases. You handle your product logic and UI customization.

The time-to-market difference is not incremental — it is an order of magnitude. And when you factor in the cost of engineering hours versus a predictable monthly subscription, the math almost always favors the SDK approach.
For a step-by-step walkthrough, check our
how to use CometChat
tutorial.
Getting Started With CometChat Today
If you are even slightly curious, the best move is to spin up the free Build plan and see for yourself. Here is a quick roadmap:
Sign up
at
cometchat.com
— no credit card needed.
Pick your platform
— web, mobile, or both. Grab the SDK for your framework.
Follow the quickstart guide
— their docs walk you through initialization, adding a chat component, and making your first message send.
Layer on features
— add video calling, configure notifications, set up moderation rules, and integrate your AI agent.
Test with real users
— the Build plan supports up to 100 users, which is plenty for internal testing and early beta feedback.
Upgrade when you are ready
— move to Basic or Advanced as your user base grows.
The entire flow from zero to a working chat prototype can happen in a single afternoon. I have done it. It works.

Want to go deeper? Here are some related guides we have published:

— A detailed comparison that highlights where CometChat excels.

— Real examples of how teams are using the SDK in production.

— Get the most out of your integration with these expert recommendations.

— A hands-on guide for your first implementation.

CometChat keeps shipping features that matter — AI agents, smarter moderation, better notifications, real analytics — without overcomplicating the pricing or the integration process. If real-time communication is part of your product roadmap, this is one of those tools where the ROI is obvious within the first week of building. Give the free plan a spin and see how fast your app goes from silent to connected.

children.text: Is CometChat really free to start?
Yes. CometChat's Build plan is completely free and includes access to all features for development and testing, with support for up to 100 users. No credit card is required to sign up.
Can CometChat handle voice and video calls, or just text chat?
CometChat supports real-time text messaging, voice calls, video calls, screen sharing, and call recording — all within a single SDK. You do not need a separate WebRTC provider.
How does CometChat's AI moderation work?

CometChat uses AI-powered content moderation that understands context, not just keywords. It flags or blocks toxic, spammy, or inappropriate messages in real time, reducing false positives and keeping communities healthy without manual oversight.
